health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Public Health Informatics
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Application of informatics principles to public health practice including disease surveillance, health promotion, and population health management. Students learn to use technology and data analytics to address community health challenges and improve population outcomes.
learning_objectives: Apply informatics tools to public health surveillance
Develop population health monitoring and reporting systems
Understand health information exchange for public health
Implement community health assessment and intervention programs
Evaluate public health informatics initiatives and outcomes
covered_materials: Public health surveillance systems and methods
Geographic information systems (GIS) in public health
Social determinants of health and data analysis
Health promotion and behavior change technologies
Global health informatics and international standards

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Healthcare Compliance and Regulations
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Comprehensive study of healthcare regulations, compliance requirements, and legal frameworks affecting health informatics. Students learn to navigate complex regulatory environments and ensure organizational compliance with healthcare laws and standards.
learning_objectives: Understand major healthcare regulations and compliance requirements
Implement HIPAA privacy and security compliance programs
Evaluate FDA regulations for health technology
Develop compliance monitoring and audit programs
Assess international healthcare regulations and standards
covered_materials: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Security Rule compliance
FDA regulations for medical devices and software
Legal and ethical considerations in health informatics

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Health Information Exchange
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Study of health information exchange architectures, standards, and implementation strategies. Students learn to facilitate secure and efficient sharing of health information across healthcare organizations and systems.
learning_objectives: Understand health information exchange models and architectures
Implement interoperability standards and protocols
Develop data sharing agreements and governance frameworks
Evaluate HIE technology solutions and platforms
Assess the impact of HIE on care coordination and outcomes
covered_materials: Health information exchange models and governance
Data sharing agreements and consent management
Patient identity management and record matching
Privacy and security in health information exchange
Care coordination and clinical workflow integration

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Healthcare Project Management
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Application of project management principles to healthcare informatics projects including system implementations, process improvements, and technology initiatives. Students learn specialized skills for managing complex healthcare projects.
learning_objectives: Apply project management methodologies to healthcare settings
Manage healthcare system implementation projects
Understand change management in healthcare organizations
Develop stakeholder engagement strategies for clinical staff
Evaluate project success in healthcare environments
covered_materials: Stakeholder management with clinical and administrative staff
Change management and user adoption strategies
Budget and resource management for health IT projects

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Research Methods in Health Informatics
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Research methodologies and statistical techniques specific to health informatics including study design, data collection, and analysis of health information systems and interventions. Students learn to conduct rigorous research in health informatics.
learning_objectives: Design health informatics research studies
Apply appropriate statistical methods to health data
Conduct systematic reviews and meta-analyses
Understand research ethics in health informatics
Evaluate and interpret health informatics research literature
covered_materials: Research design and methodology in health informatics
Usability studies and user experience research

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Human Biology
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to human biological systems including cellular biology, genetics, and basic physiological processes. Students develop foundational knowledge of human biology essential for understanding health informatics applications.
learning_objectives: Understand basic cellular and molecular biology concepts
Explain human genetics and heredity principles
Describe major organ systems and their functions
Analyze biological processes and homeostasis
Apply biological concepts to health informatics contexts
covered_materials: Biological basis of health and disease

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Anatomy and Physiology
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Detailed study of human anatomy and physiological processes including structure-function relationships across body systems. Students gain comprehensive understanding of human body systems essential for health informatics applications.
learning_objectives: Identify anatomical structures and their relationships
Explain physiological processes and mechanisms
Understand homeostatic regulation and feedback systems
Analyze clinical correlations and disease processes
Apply anatomical and physiological knowledge to health informatics
covered_materials: Respiratory system anatomy and gas exchange

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Programming for Health Informatics
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to programming concepts and languages commonly used in health informatics including database programming, data analysis, and healthcare application development. Students learn programming skills specific to healthcare contexts.
learning_objectives: Write programs for healthcare data analysis
Implement database queries for health information systems
Develop basic healthcare applications
Apply programming concepts to health informatics problems
Understand healthcare data formats and standards
covered_materials: Database programming and SQL for health data

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Biostatistics
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Statistical methods applied to biological and health data including hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and epidemiological statistics. Students learn to analyze health data and interpret statistical results in healthcare contexts.
learning_objectives: Apply statistical methods to health and biological data
Conduct hypothesis testing for clinical research
Perform regression analysis and correlation studies
Understand epidemiological measures and study designs
Interpret and communicate statistical results in healthcare

health_informatics_remaining_courses.title: Healthcare Ethics
health_informatics_remaining_courses.description: Exploration of ethical principles and dilemmas in healthcare including patient autonomy, beneficence, justice, and confidentiality. Students learn to analyze ethical issues and make principled decisions in healthcare informatics contexts.
learning_objectives: Apply ethical principles to healthcare decision-making
Analyze ethical dilemmas in health informatics
Understand patient rights and healthcare provider obligations
Evaluate ethical implications of health technology
Develop ethical frameworks for informatics practice
covered_materials: Ethical issues in health information technology
Research ethics and human subjects protection
